Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.town_maradon}Maradon
Settlement.text: {=LqPJY4s9}Maradon is the Saldaean capital. It is situated along the Arinelle River. It sits on the former site of Deranbar, the capital of Jaramide, one of the Ten Nations. Maradon is famed for its great towers, and the stunning Cordamora Palace where the rulers of Saldaea reside.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_maradon_1}Abasar
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_maradon_1}Abasar sits in the warm, oak-covered Mountains of Dhoom near the Blight. Sheep-raising is a way of life in these parts, as is sheep-rustling.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_maradon_2}Chegem
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_maradon_2}Chegem lies on the River Arinelle. The waters of the river, which are very shallow and muddy here, are well-stocked with flounder and crayfish, while trout can be fished in the muddy shallows.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_maradon_3}Gagarin
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_maradon_3}Gagarin sits beneath a small woodland looking east at the Mountains of Dhoom. As in many villages around here, the foresters of his region still defend the border from Trolloc raids.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.town_bashere}Bashere
Settlement.text: {=EJZLzhJk}Bashere is a small Saldaean settlement in the southern region of the kingdom. Bashere is said to be named for House Bashere who have a long history in this area.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_bashere_1}Tyr
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_bashere_1}Tyr sits on the western banks of the Arinelle River. Wheat grows here, fed by the ample rains of the north.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_bashere_2}Sidona
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_bashere_2}Sidona sits on the Eastern banks on the Arinelle Rivers. Any further east, the land becomes alkaline and barren, but here the floodplains allow the growing of wheat.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_bashere_3}Kayacun
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_bashere_3}Kayacun lies nestled amid the Banikhan Mountain ridges of the sourthern reaches of Saldaea. The highland soils make fine grazing for horses.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_saldaea_1_1}Kalach
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_saldaea_1_1}Kalach sits near the west of the Plain of Lances. Villagers here raise cattle bred to withstand both cold winters and hot summer.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_saldaea_1_2}Mezan
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_K6_4}Mezan lies in a narrow, dry valley at the western edges of the Plain of Lances. The peoples of this land buy horses from the nomads to their east and sell them to the Saldaean aristocracy in the towns.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_saldaea_2_1}Orsar
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_saldaea_2_1}Orsar sits in the rocky edge of the Banikhan Mountains before it gives way to barren gravel plains and dunes. Clouds from the Aryth Ocean drop their last reserves of water here in the spring and winter, giving the villages respite from the dusty mines.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.town_chachin}Chachin
Settlement.text: {=rGXdJybe}Chachin is the capital city of Kandor and has a high enough altitude to cause some difficulty in breathing and dizziness. It is located on the junction of several trade routes, including the cross-Borderland route running from Bandar Eban to Fal Moran and another running south to Tar Valon. It is located just south of the Blight and as a result is extremely heavily defended.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_chachin_1}Neya
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_chachin_1}Neya sits in the flat reaches of the Plain of Lances. This land has been good horse country since even before the Borderlands came to be, now furnishing the steeds of the Lances and Horse Archers alike.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_chachin_2}South Metiler
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_chachin_2}South Metiler sits where the plains of Lances slowly ascend to meet the Arielle River tributories. Depressions lying in the shadow of the rock remain wet for most of the year, making for good land in which to grow flax.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_chachin_3}Pechora
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_chachin_3}Pechora sits in the middle of the Plain of Lances. Swine forage in the scrubby landscape for roots.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.town_canluum}Canluum
Settlement.text: {=kghCLS9q}Canluum is a small city located in the kingdom of Kandor, close to the western border with Arafel on the main road to Chachin, capital of Kandor. The Blight is virtually on the city's doorstep with raids by Trollocs being known to occur much deeper into Kandor than Canluum.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_canluum_1}Manala
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_canluum_1}Manala sits on a plataeu near Canluum. Though the climate is relatively dry here, artesian wells allow farmers to grow bountiful crops of wheat.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_canluum_2}Kusar
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_canluum_2}Kusar, the new village, sits in the Proska Flats. The local landowners have established stud farms, supplying horses to the Kandor Armies.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_canluum_3}Lugan
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_canluum_3}Lugan sits near the Blight border. Though the climate is relatively dry here, artesian wells allow farmers to grow bountiful crops of wheat.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_kandor_1_1}Salavat
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_kandor_1_1}Salavat sits at the foot of the Mountains of Dhoom on the Proska Flats. The villagers, who until recently were nomads, raise sheep known for the long, fine wool.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_kandor_2_1}Denhuir
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_kandor_2_1}Denhuir lies on the western edges of Kandor. The hills of this district are terraced with vineyards, producing a dry wine popular throughout the Borderlands.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_kandor_2_2}Ravinda
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_kandor_2_2}Ravinda sits on the Plain of Lances. The sandy soil and morning mists, allows the growing of grapes overlooking the valleys.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.town_arbela}Shol Arbela
Settlement.text: {=ytBRXqt8}Shol Arbela, is the capital city of Arafel, known as the City of Ten Thousand Bells. It is an Arafellin custom for men and women to wear bells in their hair, though it is not known whether this is something more connected with Shol Arbela itself or the nation as a whole.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_arbela_1}Salair
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_arbela_1}Salair lies on the northern border near the Blight. Though farming is poor in these parts, villagers have constructed salt pans that stick far out into the blight tainted lands.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_arbela_2}Kolar
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_arbela_2}Kolar sits near the Mora river. The dark, well-watered soil here makes this region an Arafellian breadbasket.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_arbela_3}Medyn
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_arbela_3}Medyn sits near the Blightborder in the warm coastal hills. The dark, well-watered soil here makes this region an Arafellian breadbasket.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.town_jakanda}Jakanda
Settlement.text: {=JhBSnWAe}Little is known of the Arafel town of Jakanda. Its seems positioned as a fallback settlement on the roads to Shiener.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_jakanda_1}Peveka
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_jakanda_1}Peveka sits on a high bluff overlooking the River Mora. The sides of the hills are terraced to grow wheat, with cisterns to catch the rain.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_jakanda_2}Revdalha
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_jakanda_2}Revdalha lies near a rich vein of silver in hills overlooking the Mora river. The Arafel have minted its coins from these mines for centuries.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_jakanda_3}Gorien Springs
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_jakanda_3}Gorien Springs sits near the Fields of Merilor. Here old salt deposits sit underground from what must have once been a vast sea.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_arafel_1_1}Sibay
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_arafel_1_1}Sibay sits on a ridge south of Shol Arbela. The streams rushing down from the mountains have exposed deposits of iron in the hillsides, which are mined by the local population.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_arafel_1_2}Tarusa
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_arafel_1_2}Tarusa sits on a ridge near Tifans Well. The people of these parts have raised cattle since pre-Trolloc War times, and its milk and butter are sought out by traders and travellers passing through the region.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_arafel_2_1}Nurlat
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_arafel_2_1}The village of Nurlat sits near the Blightland border. Villagers here have laid out salt pans near where the blight has dried out the land leaving a thin crust of salt.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.town_falmoran}Fal Moran
Settlement.text: {=WGtzBswe}The natural fortress of Fal Moran holds the Royal Palace of Shienar and is considered the Capital. Fal Moran is the designed to make it easy for its citizens to retreat into in times or peril.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_falmoran_1}Mallard's Hill
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_falmoran_1}Mallard's Hill sits on a low ridge in the middle of the Shienar plains. This district is one of the rockier and less fertile parts of the plain, but the valley scrub still supports the grazing of sheep.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_falmoran_2}Oak Water
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_falmoran_2}Oak Water sits in the southern regions, protected by the fortress cities to the north. River irrigation and the relatively warm inland climate ensure that the grain fields of this district, feed much of the Shienar Kingdom.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_falmoran_3}Ankor Dail
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_falmoran_3}Ankor Dail lies in the east of the Shienar plain. The slowly rising land here gets more rain than other parts of the plain, and the rich grasses have made it good horse country.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.town_faldara}Fal Dara
Settlement.text: {=xkkI7ejQ}Fal Dara is the northernmost city in Shienar, close to the Blightborder, and part of the final line of defense between it and the cities and farmsteads farther south.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_faldara_1}Fal Noda
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_faldara_1}Fal Noda lies by the Mora River. Villagers scoop clay from the creek-bottoms here, selling it to the pottery kilns of Fal Dara and other nearby towns.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_faldara_2}Mos Fudai
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_faldara_2}Mos Fudai sits close to Tarwins Gap and nearest the threats that spawn from the blight. Villagers harvest salt from the blighted lands. These lands are dangerous, the air thick and fetid, but the inhabitants are proud of their role keeping watch on the Blight.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_faldara_3}Medo
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_faldara_3}Medo sits just south of the River Mora, right before it enters the lands of the Arafel. The inhabitants of this region have bred sheep that feed on marsh-grasses.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_shiener_1_1}Fal Sion
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_shiener_1_1}Fal Sion is a fishing village on the river banks. The nearby castle guards one of the main passages to the Aiel Waste.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_shiener_1_2}Mos Shirare
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_shiener_1_2}Mos Shirare sits between the River Erinin and the Aiel Waste. Here, the river has just descended from the coastal hills and has not yet reached the alkali plains of the southwest. The lands around produce a fine crop of grain.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_shiener_2_1}Kasai Caan
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_shiener_2_1}Kasai Caan sits in the Field of Talidar. River irrigation and the relatively warm inland climate ensure that the grain fields of this district, along with neighboring Oak Water, feed much of the Shienar Kingdom.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.town_seven}The Seven Towers
Settlement.text: {=bPDqyqkb}The Seven Towers was once the fabled capital of the proud nation of Malkier, conquered and broken by Shadowspawn armies in the last Trolloc wars.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_seven_1}Qichen
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_seven_1}Qichen sits where the waters of the Mora river meet the Blight. The villages have been enslaved and are forced to mine nearby salt deposits.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_seven_2}Adashie
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_seven_2}Adashie sits atop a western spur of Tarwin Gap. The reddish streaks in the soil and rocks testify to rich iron deposits, which are mined by the enslaved villagers.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_seven_3}Iwasei
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_seven_3}The fishing village of Iwasei sits on the eastern banks of River Mora. Prisoners set nets in the nearby river to catch a variety of fish.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.town_herots}Herot's Crossing
Settlement.text: {=T2E4xKVn}Herot's Crossing is famed as the site of the final Malkieri stand, when King al'Akir Mandragoran and Queen el'Leanna Mandragoran led the last remnants of the Malkieri army against the Shadowspawn invasion. Despite a valiant defense, the Malkieri were slaughtered and the Kingdom overrun.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_herots_1}Akrane
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_herots_1}Akrane sits on a plataeu overlooking Tarwins Gap. Darkfriends oversee prisoners who tend the groves of olives that thrive here.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_herots_2}Onehira
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_herots_2}Onehira sits at a plateau in the Blight. Sheltered somewhat by a high ridge to their southeast, the darkfriends here are raise barley and grain.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_herots_3}Hiratai
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_herots_3}Hiratai sits at the foot of one the strange Blight forests. Trollocs are sent to the nearby woods to harvest one of the Blights few sources of timber.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_malkier_1_1}Kariwahen
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_malkier_1_1}Kariwahen lies in the far north of the old Malkieri lands, long absorbed by blight. The sheep here bear unusually thick wool, as there are few trees or hills to protect them from the icy winter winds blowing down the frozen northeastern steppe.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_malkier_1_2}Niwafune
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_malkier_1_2}Niwafune lies in the far north of the old Malkieri lands, long absorbed by blight. Though the winters are cold, the spring grasses are fine grazing for the local sheep, whose thick wool protects them from the region's piercing winds.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_malkier_2_1}Sannohe
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_malkier_2_1}Sannohe sits overlooking a tributary of the River Mora. This district specializes in the harvest of timber which is floated on the river and sold to the lake towns.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_shadow_1_1}Twighlight Tannery
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_shadow_1_1}Twighlight Tannery provides furs and pelts to be transformed into clothing and trolloc armours.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_shadow_2_1}Shredded Sawmill
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_shadow_2_1}Shredded Sawmill provides lumber to the Shadow cause.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_shadow_2_2}Gallowshill Stable
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_shadow_2_2}Gallowshill Stable produces horses for the Shadows forces. Horses that don't meet the requirements end up in the trolloc camps.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_shadow_3_1}Thornwall Weavery
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_shadow_3_1}Thornwall Weavery produces thread, twine and rope for the Shadow.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_shadow_4_1}Deadwood Abatoir
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_shadow_4_1}Deadwood Abatoir produces large quantities of meat to supply trollocs with.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_shadow_4_2}Blackwater Trappers
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_shadow_4_2}Blackwater Trappers provide the furs and skins the local tanneries need to run their enterprises with.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_shadow_5_1}Bleakmore Ranch
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_shadow_5_1}Bleakmore Ranch provides milk, butter, meat and hides to feed the shadow horde.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_shadow_5_2}Grimsand Crop
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_shadow_5_2}Grimsand Crop is that of sweet dates. Energy and fuel for darkfriends and dreadlords.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_shadow_6_1}Forsaken Butchery
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_shadow_6_1}Forsaken Butchery prides itself on the finest cuts, supplying the Darkfriends first and Trollocs second.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_shadow_6_2}Betrayed Stud
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_shadow_6_2}Betrayed Stud bred the finest horses that the elite forces of the Shadow demand.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_shadow_7_1}Curdled Plantation
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_shadow_7_1}Curdled Plantation provides sugary dates to the darkfriends that support the Shadows cause.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_shadow_7_2}Eyeless Feedlot
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_S5_3}Eyeless Feedlot produces large quantities of grain that the various Shadow farms require to feed the animals that in turn, feed trollocs.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_shadow_8_1}Dreamsong Yard
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_shadow_8_1}Dreamsong Yard stores the lumber harvested from the Blight forests.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_shadow_9_1}Ravenshollow Forge
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_shadow_9_1}Ravenshollow Forge uses both local ore and imported iron to produce the large quantities of blades the Shadows war machine needs.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_shadow_9_2}Bonehaven Stable
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_shadow_9_2}Bonehaven Stable produces the finest of stock. These are horses fit for the Forsaken themselves.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_shadow_10_1}Shadowmere Grove
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_shadow_10_1}Shadowmere Grove harvests the local olives, a staple of the Darkfriends in the Blight.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_shadow_11_1}Thornwall Oasis
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_shadow_11_1}Thornwall Oasis harvests dates suitable for pulp. This pulp is made into rations for the Darkfriends as a long life ration.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.town_shayol}Shayol Ghul
Settlement.text: {=i2VkRLBg}Shayol Ghul the focal point of the Dark One's power. It is located in the lifeless Blasted Lands, a place which bore the brunt of the War of Power. The Blasted Lands are inaccessible for the most part, for they are past the Great Blight. Entire armies have died fighting the Blight.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_shayol_1}Nightshade Winery
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_shayol_1}Nightshade Winery produces delicates wines for Darkfriends and Forsaken to enjoy.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_shayol_2}Shadow Mill
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_shayol_2}Shadow Mill produces grain to provide to the Shadows Forces.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_shayol_3}Hollow Grove
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_shayol_3}Hollow Grove produces olives for local presses. Oil from which has many uses in the Shadow Spawn forces.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.town_sherandu}Sherandu
Settlement.text: {=PPadNxa6}Sherandu is a staging point for Myrddraal where their masters assign them companies of Trollocs to control.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_sherandu_1}Foulwood Ranch
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_sherandu_1}Foulwood Ranch provides the meat to fuel the Trolloc hordes on their way south.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_sherandu_2}Foulearth Farm
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_sherandu_2}Foulearth Farm is yet another meat producer for the trolloc horde.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_sherandu_3}Murkroot Sawmill
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_sherandu_3}Murkroot Sawmill provides lumber to the Shadow, source from the nearby Blight forests.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.town_fortress}The Fortress
Settlement.text: {=Xom9DeXZ}The Fortress provides the Shadow a collection point for their forces to build and launch armed legions of Trollocs, Myrddraal, Dreadlords and worse.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_fortress_1}Dreadmill Abatoir
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_fortress_1}Dreadmill Abatoir needs to supply The Fortress with meat for the Trolloc forces amassed within.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_fortress_2}Wraithstone Smithy
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_fortress_2}Wraithstone Smithy takes the local ore and churns out weapons enmasse for the Shadow forces.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_fortress_3}Cinder Churn
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_fortress_3}Cinder Churn produces butter and milk products to supplent the meat from Dreadmill Abatoir.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.town_thetown}The Town
Settlement.text: {=aTMypBiY}The Town is inhabited mainly by Darkfriends and the numerous people enslaved by them. It is a very dangerous place where the laws of force, fear, and crime are rule. Despite its reputation, it has existed for over 2000 years.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_thetown_1}Smokey Press
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_thetown_1}Smokey Press produces oil from the local olives.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_thetown_2}Gallows Foundry
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_thetown_2}Gallows Foundry produces iron from the local ore to be shipped to the Shadow forges around the Blight.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_thetown_3}Bleakharvest Piggery
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_thetown_3}Bleakharvest Piggery raises hogs for the local Town and its residents.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.town_thakandar}Thakan'dar
Settlement.text: {=pbHXtJ82}Thakan'dar, where shadow-forgers forge the tainted swords of Myrddraal at the cost of human souls. Lightning flashes up at the clouds in this place. There is no life, and it is as cold as the ice to the north, yet the place is dry as any desert.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_thakandar_1}Obsidion Distillery
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_thakandar_1}Obsidion Distillery uses the local grains to produce a firey beverage the trollocs are rewarded with.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_thakandar_2}Blighted Cultivation
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_thakandar_2}Blighted Cultivation produces olives for preserving, to sustain darkfriends on their long marches south.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_thakandar_3}Corrupted Forge
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_thakandar_3}Corrupted Forge consumes local ores to produce the iron needed by the Shadows war machine.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.town_doom}Pit of Doom
Settlement.text: {=ygJkPxyc}The Pit of Doom is the only place where the Pattern is thin enough to make the Bore detectable. The sky seen here contains clouds of multiple colors that are seen to race across the sky with impossible speed. This is where the Dreadlords swear oaths to the Dark One.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_doom_1}Shadewood Stud
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_doom_1}Shadewood Stud breds the horse the Myrddraal choose to ride.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_doom_2}Blackened Station
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_doom_2}Blackened Station provides meat first and foremost to feed trollocs. The wool is a byproduct used in clothing in these parts.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_doom_3}Blackroot Basin
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_doom_3}Blackroot Basin is a glorified saltmine, bring salt to preserve foods for the Shadow Forces.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.town_jumaras}Jumaras Nest
Settlement.text: {=Dxnj5JQE}Jumaras Nest is home to Aginors labs that produced the wormlike creatures found in the blight. It is said they can live for thousands of years and actually get stronger when the One power is used against them.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_jumaras_1}Rottenmarsh Ranch
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_jumaras_1}Rottenmarsh Ranch is one of the many meat suppliers for the Trolloc armies.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_jumaras_2}Boneyard Ring
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_jumaras_2}Boneyard Ring trains the horse used by the Myrddraal.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_jumaras_3}Slavers Butchery
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_jumaras_3}Slavers Butchery provides even more meat to the Trolloc forces.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.town_shadow_3}Gholams Perch
Settlement.text: {=F5HlQdgf}Gholams Perch is named after another of Aginors creations. Created to assisinate Aes Sedai, they are resistant to the One power and most known weapons.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_gholams_1}Cursed Shear
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_gholams_1}Cursed Shear is a shearing shed that produces wool and the occasional lamb roast for the local population.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_gholams_2}Ruined Churn
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_gholams_2}Ruined Churn produces the regions milk, butter and cheeses.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_gholams_3}Broken Rack
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_gholams_3}Broken Rack cures hides for clothing and light armours.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.town_draghkar}Draghkar Kiss
Settlement.text: {=uO4dxCl8}Draghkar were created by Dreadlords rather than Aginor. They are used as assasins and overseers by the Shadow.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_draghkar_1}Shattered Cereal
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_draghkar_1}Shattered Cereal produces grains for the mills of the Shadow.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_draghkar_2}Despair Mill
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_draghkar_2}Despair Mill produces flours from the local grains and those from farms such as Shattered Cereal nearby.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_draghkar_3}Nightfall Orchard
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_draghkar_3}Nightfall Orchard produces sweet dates to provide comfort to the Samma N'Sei Aiel channelers.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.town_whitebridge}Whitebridge
Settlement.text: {=8UJ3cPrZ}Whitebridge is a town in central-western Andor, named for the huge milk-white bridge that spans the River Arinelle. The bridge is believed to date from the Age of Legends, and looks to be made of impossibly fragile white glass, yet is so strong even a chisel and hammer will not mark it. The bridge location makes Whitebridge a logical stop for trade carvans and travellers alike.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_whitebridge_1}Kingscroft
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_whitebridge_1}Kingscroft sits in the plains east of Whitebridge. Although mostly covered with beech and oak woods, the mountain slopes provide good grazing for horses and has traditionally been home to stud farms for cavalry commanders.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_whitebridge_2}Stromwatch
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_whitebridge_2}Stromwatch, a relatively recent settlement, was named by an Andoran captain after the dawn for its fine westward views of Whitebridge. It was founded as a stud farm to breed imperial horses with steppe stock from the east, and continues to provide horses for cavalry commanders.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_whitebridge_3}Swine Hollows
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_whitebridge_3}Swine Hollows sits upon sun-drenched banks of River Arinelle. Stys have been built to allow the growing of hogs for the local Whitebridge markets.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.town_baerlon}Baerlon
Settlement.text: {=nhmBQIC4}Baerlon sits near the Mountains of Mist far to the West of the Andor capital. The city is used as a trading and warehouseing hub for the outputs of village enterprises in the region. Its large gates only open during daylight hours to deter raiders from attempting to pillage its stored commodities.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_baerlon_1}Brenwood
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_baerlon_1}Brenwood sits near the steep wooded slopes of the Mountains of Mist, overlooking Baerlon. Villagers harvest the tall trees of the heights, selling the logs to the cities of the Westlands.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_baerlon_2}Kilnspire
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_baerlon_2}Kilnspire sits on a short river that rushes out of the Mountains of Mist. The river slows as it hits a nearby bend, allowing the collection of potters' clay from the banks.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_baerlon_3}Goldharvest
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_baerlon_3}Goldharvest sits on the flatlands north of Baerlon, where the warmth of the Andorian plains begins to give way to the chills coming off the Mountains of Mist. Villagers plant grains to feed the urban population of Baerlon.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.town_emonds}Emonds Field
Settlement.text: {=1SnAH5K8}Emonds Field was originally named Aemons Field after the Manetheron King who fought his deperate last stand here during the Trolloc Wars. Legend holds that when these heroes of Manetheron died, a spring identical to the Winespring burst forth where they fell to forever mark their sacrifice. It seems a fitting capital for the Dragon Reborn and his followers.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_emonds_1}Orebarrow
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_emonds_1}Orebarrow sits in the center of the foothills of the Mountains of Mist. The soil here is thin and the weather here is cold, but rich iron deposits sustain the local economy.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_emonds_2}Glenharrow
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_emonds_2}Glenharrow was once a marshy wasteland, but Manetheron settlers drained the waters and sowed wheat and rye in the valley.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_emonds_3}Morgrim
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_emonds_3}Morgrim lies in a series of small valleys that lead out of the Moutains. The people of this land are sheep herders, known for their dry, salty cheese flavored with cumin.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_dragon_1_1}Ravenscroft
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_dragon_1_1}Ravenscroft sits east Taren Ferry. The hillsides here are terraced for the growing of grapes, which produce a heavy, rich vintage that the wealthy drink mixed with honey and cloves, and the common folk with water.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_dragon_1_2}Dugmund
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_dragon_1_2}Dugmund sits overlooking the River Manetherendrelle. Villagers collect clay from the riverbanks for the kilns of the artisan potters.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_dragon_2_1}Moonhaven
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_dragon_2_1}Moonhaven sits in the cold misty foothils that rise into the Mountains of Mist. The low-lying lands are sheltered and suitable for growing hardy grains.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_dragon_2_2}Carrmound
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_dragon_2_2}Carrmound lies on the forest trails that lead to Emond's field. Villagers here dig a fine pale clay, rich in mica and feldspar, from the mountain streams.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_dragon_3_1}Vinespire
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_dragon_3_1}Vinespire in the middle of the Western valley that leads to the Almoth Plain. Villagers plant olives here on the hillsides.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_dragon_3_2}Iron Hills
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_dragon_3_2}Iron Hills takes its name from the ruddy, iron-rich soil of this part of Andor. The mines of this district supply the iron ore for many of the forges of the kingdom.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_dragon_4_1}Lowbridge
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_dragon_4_1}Lowbridge sits on the wooded plains north of Whitebridge and northwest of Deven Ride. Villagers here grow wheat in the gentle climate.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_dragon_4_2}Brightwood
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_dragon_4_2}Brightwood lies below the slopes of the western Plains, north of Deven Ride. Misty mornings and warm afternoons make this land well-suited to the growing of grapes.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.town_ashaman}The Black Tower
Settlement.text: {=UHvEEEBb}The Black Tower is the headquarters of the Asha'man, the organization of male channelers started by Rand al'Thor and trained by Mazrim Taim. It originated as a converted farmhouse located less than two leagues south of Caemlyn.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_ashaman_1}Tower Farms
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_ashaman_1}Tower Farms are one of the major grain producing centers for the Black Tower.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_ashaman_2}Tower Wharf
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_ashaman_2}Tower Wharf is a fishing village responsible for bringing fish such as perch and bream back to the Black Tower.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_ashaman_3}Tower Feilds
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_ashaman_3}Tower Feilds is the second grain producing farm supplying staples to the Black Tower.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_ashaman_4}Tower Pens
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_ashaman_4}Tower Pens produce the Black Towers meat, predominatly pigs which require small areas to produce large numbers.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.town_ES4}Caemlyn
Settlement.text: {=TI3fS2xN}Caemlyn is one of the most beautiful and largest cities in the Westlands. Like a gleaming crown upon its gently rolling hills, the city is actually made up of two sections, the New City, built well under two thousand years ago by the hands of men, and the ancient Inner City, much of which bears the mark of Ogier stonemasons.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_caemlyn_1}Buryhill
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_caemlyn_1}Buryhill sits on the plains nor-east of Caemlyn. Villagers here produce the spicy red grapes that are used to decant the fortified wines of Andoran nobles.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_caemlyn_2}Breen's Spring
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_caemlyn_2}Breen's Spring sits nor-west of Caemlyn. Named after the local wells nickname, the landowners have turned over the pastures to the raising of far more profitable horses.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_caemlyn_3}Carysford
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_caemlyn_3}Carysford sits through the Far Madding gate, south of Caemlyn. Villagers divert the mountain streams to irrigate their rich grainfields.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.town_braem}New Braem
Settlement.text: {=SUnaVliN}New Braem is located in Braem wood and acts as a trading center are stop over for those heading to and from Tar Valon. The town is built near the ruins of Braem, an Ogier-built city of Coremanda, one of the Ten Nations destroyed in the Trolloc Wars.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_braem_1}Millers March
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_braem_1}Millers March sits in the narrow plain between Braem Woods and Tar Valon. This land is fine country for wheat.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_braem_2}Galden Field
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_braem_2}Galden Field is a village at the center of a wheat-growing district on a narrow stretch of plain between New Braem and Tar Valon.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_braem_3}Market Sheran
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_braem_3}Market Sheran is a village on the edge of the Braem Woods. The villagers here make most of their income from the raising of sheep.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.town_aringill}Aringill
Settlement.text: {=5SpYVtie}Aringill is a moderately-sized port town on the west bank of the River Erinin, on the border with Cairhien. Aringill is Andor's largest and most important port, linking it by water with the city of Tar Valon upriver and with Tear downriver.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_aringill_1}Maerone
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_aringill_1}Maerone sits on the east bank of River Erinin, making it techinically in Cairhien territory. The locals however, consider Aringill their local authority, causing some political tension between the two nations. The villagers raise cattle in the highland pastures.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_aringill_2}Sheldyn
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_aringill_2}Sheldyn lies alongside the River Erinin. This district is one of the grain breadbaskets of Andor.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_aringill_3}Chisen Feilds
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_aringill_3}Chisen Feilds sits in the upper valley near the Chisen Hills. This district is one of the grain breadbaskets of Andor.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_andor_1_1}Arien
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_andor_1_1}Arien sits on the westward Caemlyn road to Whitebridge. Fine clay is collected from the banks of the nearby river.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_andor_2_1}Cullens Crossing
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_andor_2_1}The village of Cullens Crossing sits near one of the many feeder streams to the River Erinin. The land is best known for the horses that are grazed on the desert's short-lived but rich spring grasses.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_andor_2_2}Jornhill
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_andor_2_2}The village of Jornhill is near the northern border od the Andor lands. The people of these parts have long reared the finest horse for the kingdom.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_andor_3_1}Forel Market
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_andor_3_1}Forel Market lies on a plateau overlooking the River Erinin in the distance. Villagers grow olives on the slopes, famous for their light, fragrant oil.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_andor_3_2}Kore Springs
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_andor_3_2}Kore Springs sits south of Braem Woods. Wine grapes grow on the gentle slopes rising up from the river valley.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_andor_4_1}Harlon Bridge
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_andor_4_1}Harlon Bridge sits next to the River Storn. Early morning mists from the west and the hot sun of midday allow the growing of wine grapes.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_andor_4_2}Danabar
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_andor_4_2}Danabar sits on the Ludgard Road, between Four Kings and Damelien. Iron ore mines sit on the slopes of the nearby hills, providing the Andoran foundrys with material.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.town_tarvalon}Tar Valon
Settlement.text: {=YYYxOiCq}Tar Valon is located on the River Erinin, within sight of Dragonmount. It is the center of Aes Sedai power and is also the second largest, most populous city in the Westlands. Tar Valon is a vital trade and transport link between the Borderlands and the more populous, richer southern kingdoms of Andor, Cairhien, Illian, and Tear.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_tarvalon_1}Jualdhe
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_tarvalon_1}Jualdhe sits at the foot of the Dragonmount. Villagers have cleared away the thickets and thornbrush of this region, making space for hogs to feed the large population of Tar Valon.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_tarvalon_2}Darein
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_tarvalon_2}Darein lies next to the River Erinin. The lowlands here are suited to the growing of flax.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_tarvalon_3}Alindaer
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_tarvalon_3}Alindaer sits overlooking a pass between the Dragonmount and River Erinin. The villagers of this region specializing in the raising of pigs.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_sedai_1_1}Daghain
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_sedai_1_1}Daghain in the valley between the lands of Andor and Dragonmount. The location makes a great base for fur trappers with mountains to the north and plains to the south.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_sedai_1_2}Lugagde
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_sedai_1_2}Lugagde sits on a plateau north of the Alianelle Spring Fort. Cattle are raised here in former marshy woodlands, now cleared and turned into meadows.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_sedai_2_1}Dorlan
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_sedai_2_1}Dorlan sits on the eastern bank of River Erinin. The river provides furs and the plains small game to skin.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_sedai_2_2}Osenrein
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_sedai_2_2}Osenrein is a fishing village on the River Erinin. Villagers place nets and cages in the shallow edges of the lake to catch roach, bream, perch, and crayfish.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.town_cairhien}Cairhien
Settlement.text: {=4ENGzVaC}Cairhien is a large city, originally built by Ogier, located on the banks of the River Alguenya just south of its junction with the Gaelin. The city is known primarily as the seat of the Cairhien king, for the institution of the political game Daes Dae'mar, and for a hatred above all else of the Aiel since the Aiel War.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_cairhien_1}Moutone
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_cairhien_1}Moutone lies in the broad plains near River Gaelin. This is good sheep country, but in a land scoured by fugitives, wild riders and steppe nomads, the villagers here have learned to watch their flocks closely.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_cairhien_2}Jurene
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_cairhien_2}Jurene lies near the southern end of Lake Tanaesis. The village sits near a deposit of salt, and salt springs occasionally bubble to the surface, feeding the legend that this was once a gateway to a haunted underground sea.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_cairhien_3}Maerone
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_cairhien_3}Maerone sits in a low plain near River Alguenya. The villagers mine salt from deposits beneath the ridges on the Calsean side of the pass.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.town_tremon}Tremonsien
Settlement.text: {=RL2vacqv}Tremonsien is a Cairhienen town that those unacustomed to the Daes Dae'marsmall should stop and learn before heading to the capital. Nearby their is a great excavation underway, to uncover what appears to be a giant statue.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_tremon_1}Argile
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_tremon_1}Argile lies beneath gypsum bluffs overlooking the River Erinin. The fine soil of the hills makes for a sticky clay when it washes down to the river, much prized by the potters of Cairhien.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_tremon_2}Bleville
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_tremon_2}Bleville lies alongside the gently flowing Erinin river. The low rolling hills of this district comprise one of the breadbaskets of Cairhien.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_tremon_3}Fossefer
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_tremon_3}Fossefer sits at the foot of the Western reaches of Kinslayers Dagger, a rugged range in the North of Cairhien. The silver mined from nearby hills helps explain the effort and expense to which the Cairhien Kings have gone in past centuries to conquer and defend these lands.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_cairhien_1_1}Betaille
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_cairhien_1_1}Betaille sits near River Alguenya. Highland cattle thrive on the grasses of the plains in this area.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_cairhien_1_2}Orgean
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_cairhien_1_2}Orgean lies in a valley below the Spine of the World. Despite the cold winters, the region's fertile soils make this district one of the breadbaskets of the Cairhien kingdom.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_cairhien_2_1}Terraine
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_cairhien_2_1}Terraine and its outlying hamlets sit on the lowlands to the east of Cairhien. Though rough country, it is known for the fine clay that can be gathered from the ravines.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_cairhien_3_1}Selean
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_cairhien_3_1}Selean in a narrow valley near the start of River Alguenya. Villagers here cultivate shrubs and bushes. The leaves are harvested and fed to hogs, producing meat and hides much valued by the garrisoned troops.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_cairhien_3_2}Camargue
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_cairhien_3_2}The village of Camargue and its surrounding hamlets lie on the Cairhien side of the Jangai Pass, prime grazing lands for raising horses.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.town_ludgard}Lugard
Settlement.text: {=n9WMUuSp}Lugard is the capital city of Murandy. Lugard is a dirty city, dusty and noisy, its gray outer walls in a state of disrepair and its inner stone walls in worse shape. Many of its broad roads are unpaved, despite the flourishing trade that brings scores of wagons trafficking through—from Illian, Ebou Dar, Ghealdan, and Andor.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_ludgard_1}Trustair
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_ludgard_1}Trustair lies on a narrow shelf of land between the River Reisendrelle and The Splintered Hills. The villagers are skilled miners, extracting iron ores from the nearby cliffside.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_ludgard_2}Hinderstap
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_ludgard_2}Hinderstap lies in a narrow valley beneath a towering ridge of the Splinterd Hills. The highland streams wash fertile soil down the hillsides, making this district one of the breadbaskets of the imperial north.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_ludgard_3}Remen
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_ludgard_3}Remen is a quiet fishing village on the River Manetherendrelle. Villagers harvest pike from the rivers waters.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.town_minde}Minde
Settlement.text: {=rX8oGVvV}Minde is a city in Murandy. Mindeans often boast about their bad tempers. This likely feeds the reputation that Lugard has not only for commerce but for thievery and licentiousness as well. Outsiders are well advised to heed the Lugarder saying - Trust no one but yourself, and yourself not too much.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_minde_1}Hochfeld
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_minde_1}Hochfeld sits near the River Storn. These sunny, well-watered highlands are one of the breadbaskets of Murandy.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_minde_2}Ferdendale
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_minde_2}Ferdendale sits amid rolling chalk hills between Minde and Andor. The rich grass of the down is some of the best horse-breeding land in Murandy.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_minde_3}Schafen
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_minde_3}Schafen lies under a ridge of limestone crags near the hill country known as the Hills of Kintara. The grasses on the rolling downs are well-suited to the raising of sheep.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_murandy_1_1}Viehair
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_murandy_1_1}Viehair sits in a broad valley to the south of Ludgard amongst the hill country. Nomadic herders once occupied this land, and the village's name is taken from their word for cattle.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_murandy_2_1}Eisengrube
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_murandy_2_1}Eisengrube sits in in the Splintered Hills and thrives on the ore deposits exposed where the cataract cuts through the escaparment.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_murandy_2_2}Waldheim
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_murandy_2_2}Waldheim sits near the base of the Splintered Hills. This foresting village has had mostly good relations with its Andorian neighbors thanks to decades of intermarriage, though they have become strained of late with the outbreak of war.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.town_V6}Jehannah
Settlement.text: {=R6YJsK89}Jehannah is the capital of Ghealdan and holds the location of the royal residence known as the Jheda Palace. It was built on the site of Shanaine, formerly a part of Manetheren. It lies on the River Boern.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_jehannah_1}Harel
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_jehannah_1}Harel lies on the plains to the south of the Forest of Shadows. The warm lands of the Ghealdean north are well-suited to the growing of olives.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_jehannah_2}Teresia
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_jehannah_2}Teresia shares its name with that of the former Ghealdean Queen who abdicated her crown. The warm lands of the Ghealdean north are well-suited to the growing of olives.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_jehannah_3}Jarra
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_jehannah_3}Jarra sits on a shelf overlooking the River Boern. The warm north of the Ghealdean lands is well-suited to the cultivation of olives.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.town_boannda}Boannda
Settlement.text: {=wKMo2CUP}Boannda is considered to be a large town, with three towers and a small palace. The name Boannda has an eerie resemblance to Semirhage's original name, Nemene Damendar Boann - although no one is sure what, if any, connection exists.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_boannda_1}Bethal
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_boannda_1}Bethal lies near the beginning of the River Boern. The folk of this region live well off the pike and sturgeon of the river.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_boannda_2}Samara
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_boannda_2}Samara sits in forested foothills of the Mountains of Mist. The slopes of the nearby hills are pierced with mineshafts. Despite centuries of use, they still yield ores from which silver can be extracted through various cunning techniques.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_boannda_3}Sidon
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_boannda_3}Sidon sits to the south of Boannda. The locals raise cattle in the lush pastures of the region.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_ghealdan_1_1}Willar
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_ghealdan_1_1}Willar sits near the the River Boern, just west of Samaha. Villagers grow flax in the muddy valleys between the hills in this wet area.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_ghealdan_1_2}Tallan
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_ghealdan_1_2}Tallan sits among rye and barley fields next to the River Manetherendrelle. Landowners here have turned forest-land into pasture for horses.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_ghealdan_2_1}Fyall
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_ghealdan_2_1}Fyall lies on the flat plains near the River Manetherendrelle. Ghealdan colonists have started cattle farms on the lush pastures for milk and butter.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_aiel_1_1}Shadow Peak Shade
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_aiel_1_1}Shadow Peak Shade overlooks the Colrada Valley in the shadow of the Peaks that seperate the Blight from the Aiel Waste. Aiel here grow dates in gardens watered by wells and small dams that trap run-off from the cliffs during the short rainy season.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_aiel_1_2}Colrada Valley Shade
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_aiel_1_2}Colrada Valley Shade lies at the Western edge of the Colrada Valley. Aiel farmers here grow date orchards in the oddly rich soils of this area.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_aiel_2_1}Shiagi Ridge Shade
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_aiel_2_1}Shiagi Ridge Shade is clustered around a well on a isolated wide ridge between Jangai and Niamh Passes. The surrounding lands, though dry, produce enough grass in the spring to allow the breeding of horses.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_aiel_2_2}Gandom Shade
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_aiel_2_2}Gandom Shade sits on the plateau above the Waste. This soil, gets keep moist enough in the shadow of the Mountains to produce bountiful crops of wheat.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_aiel_3_1}Hazy Ridge Shade
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_aiel_3_1}Hazy Ridge Shade sits atop a ridge overlooking Bent Valley Hold. Villagers trap rainwater and runoff from the hills to water their fields and their gardens of date-palms.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_aiel_4_1}Comarda Plateau Shade
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_aiel_4_1}Comarda Plateau Shade

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_aiel_4_2}Jangai Pass Shade
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_aiel_4_1}Jangai Pass Shade sits on a ridgeline overlooking the Jangai Pass. The wetland winds bring just enough rain to support the cultivation of olive trees.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_aiel_5_1}Shende Ridge Shade
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_aiel_5_1}Shende Ridge Shade on the edge of The Spine of the World. This ridge catches rainclouds coming off the Maraside Mountains and are brushed by downours from Haddon Mirk, allowing cistern-watered fields of millet and wheat.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_aiel_5_2}Mainde Cut Shade
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_aiel_5_2}Mainde Cut Shade lies in a nook just inside the Aiel Waste beyonf Haddon Pass, bordered to the south by the Termool desert. Villagers trap floods running down the hills during rare rainstorms, using the water to grow date palms and other crops.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_aiel_6_1}Red Springs Shade
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_aiel_6_1}Red Springs Shade sits on the path between Cold Rocks Hold and Alcair Dal. The climate is well-suited to olive trees that thrive despite the short rainy season.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_aiel_6_2}Hot Springs Shade
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_aiel_6_2}Hot Springs Shade lies to the East of Sulara. Here the Aiel raise horses to sell to Wetlanders who prize their resilience by taking advantage of the brief but lush crop of grass that covers the slopes in the spring.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_aiel_7_1}Haddon Shade
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_aiel_7_1}Haddon Shade sits beyond the Passes to Haddon mirk, well out of prying Westland eyes. Though this area is dry, it acts as a hub to provide timber to the surrounding settlements from the Wetland forests to sustain a small lumber industry.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_aiel_8_1}Uruk Shade
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_aiel_8_1}Uruk Shade sits deep in the Aiel Waste. Decent clay can be found in the small wells that tried to find the Artesian waters that feed Rhuidean.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_aiel_8_2}Vashi Shade
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_aiel_8_2}Vashi Shade sits alongside Chaendaer, and is used as a fabric trading post.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.town_aiel_1}Rhuidean
Settlement.text: {=1ImuW3My}Rhuidean was built by the Jenn Aiel after the Breaking of the World. The many ter'angreal left by the original city builders reveal the truth of the Aiel origins. As such only men wish to become clan cheifs and apprentice Wise Ones are allowed to enter the city.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_aiel_1_1}Jenn Shade
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_aiel_1_1}Jenn Shade lies on the ridgeline overlooking Rhuidean and is named after the original Aiel clan who began the construction of the city.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_aiel_1_2}Chiefs Shade
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_aiel_1_2}Chiefs Shade represents a waiting place for clans while prospective chiefs are tested in the ruins of Rhuidean.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_aiel_1_3}Wise Ones Shade
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_aiel_1_3}Wise Ones Shade represents a waiting place for Wise Ones while their apprentices are tested by the ter'angreal of Rhuidean as worthy or not of becoming Wise Ones themselves.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.town_A5}Cold Rocks Hold
Settlement.text: {=IIMWPVhU}Cold Rocks Hold was founded by the Taardad Aiel of the Nine Valleys sept. It is the last large settlement on the Silk caravan journey to the Sharan trading gates.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_aiel_2_1}Cold Rock Ridge Shade
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_aiel_2_1}Cold Rock Ridge Shade, while producing livestock on the scrub like vegetation that covers the ridges, also acts as am unassuming watch post. One can never be too complacent with the gateway to the powerful Sharan nation nearby.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_aiel_2_2}Cold Rock Valley Shade
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_aiel_2_2}Cold Rock Valley Shade sits near the oasis below Cold Rocks Hold. Farmland is limited here, and the land is best known for the horses that are grazed on the desert's short-lived but rich spring grasses. These can be traded with Silk merchants for other harder to come by resources this deep in the Aiel wastes.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_aiel_2_3}Cold Water Shade
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_aiel_2_3}Cold Water Shade sits by the Cold Rocks Oasis, well sheltered in the valley and provides the local community with abundent crops.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.town_aiel_3}Bent Valley Hold
Settlement.text: {=NFIYqaab}Bent Valley Hold overlooks, at a distance, the Jangai Pass. Bent Valley Hold draws wetlander silk caravans heading to the Sharan trade gates - away from the sacred city of Rhuidean. Aiel guides will then lead caravans north east through the dune valleys to Cold Rocks Hold.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_aiel_3_1}Silk Path Shade
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_aiel_3_1}Silk Path Shade offers save haven and rest for caravans on the perilous journey to the Silk trade gates of the Sharan lands.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_aiel_3_2}Bent Plateau Shade
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_aiel_3_2}Bent Plateau Shade provides shelter to those pit-mining in the dry nearby hills for silver.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_aiel_3_3}Bent Ridge Shade
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_aiel_3_3}Bent Ridge Shade is centered around a group of deep wells that provide dirty water - and when mixed with the unique local subsoil, allow the creation of clay to sell to the potters of the Aiel.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.town_aiel_4}Sandy Plains Hold
Settlement.text: {=bnl7Va4c}Sandy Plains Hold sits at the northern edge of the Termool Desert.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_aiel_4_1}Sandy Hollow Shade
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_aiel_4_1}Sandy Hollow Shade sits in a great depression north of Sandy Plains Hold. Villagers sink wells here to tap the groundwater, using it to irrigate their olive groves.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_aiel_4_2}Sandy Ridge Shade
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_aiel_4_2}Sandy Ridge Shade overlooks Sandy Plains Hold and the vast Temool desert. The surrounding lands, although barren and alkaline, are close enough to the Westlands for conditions to allow date-palms to thrive.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_aiel_4_3}Termool Shade
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_aiel_4_3}Termool Shade is the last campsite before the vast and unhospitable Termool Desert. The land is mostly desert-steppe. Barren most of the year except for scrub, it is surprisingly covered in grass and flowers in the spring and is fine grazing for horses. This is reportably due to the Ogier influence from Stedding Shangtai to the Southwest.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.town_aiel_5}Alcair Dal
Settlement.text: {=jXBnf2hQ}Alcair Dal is translated as The Golden Bowl from the old tongue. Alcair Dal is used as a meeting place for Aiel clan chiefs.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_aiel_5_1}Salt Bowl Shade
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_aiel_5_1}Salt Bowl Shade is the affectionate name of the salt miners settlement near Alcair Dal.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_aiel_5_2}Niamh Ridge Shade
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_aiel_5_2}Niamh Ridge Shade sits on the ridge leading out of the Aiel Waste to the Niamh Passes, where the Waste deserts give way to desert-steppe with scattered acacia and scrub. The Aiel here sink wells to water their groves of date palms.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_aiel_5_3}Sweet Tooth Shade
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_aiel_5_3}Sweet Tooth Shade is a favorite resting place of Aiel Warriors and Maidens waiting for their clan cheifs to finish their discord at nearby Alcair Dal. The abundance of extra sweet dates gives rise to the name.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.town_A7}Imre Stand
Settlement.text: {=GChIoZdd}Imre Stand marks a spring that is of great importance to herdsmen and travellers of the Aiel Waste. It is located deep in the territory of the Taardad Clan.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_aiel_6_1}Imre Spring Shade
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_A7_1}Imre Spring Shade sits next to the Imre Spring and produces bountiful crops of wheat.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_A7_2}Herdsman Shade
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_aiel_6_2}Herdsman Shade overlooks the life giving Imre spring and provides Aiel herdsman a safe resting place while grazing their herds.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_A7_3}Imre Plains Shade
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_aiel_6_3}Imre Plains Shade sits near the Imre Spring in a shelter depression in the dunes. The shelter and proximity to the spring allow the Aiel to grow wheat and other crops.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.town_mayene}Mayene
Settlement.text: {=J4bgNT0r}Mayene is a small independant city state located on an isolated penisula on the Sea of Storms. Mayene is extremely wealthly through trade of its fish oils to the Westlands, Shara and the Sea Folk.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_mayene_1}Waipara
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_mayene_1}Waipara sits on a rocky shelf overlooking the waters of Remara Bay. Groundwater trapped by the rocks is accessed by wells and used to irrigate the village wheatfields.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_mayene_2}Ika Point
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_mayene_2}Ika Point sits on the southern point of the Mayene Penisula looking out to the Sea of Storms. Villagers here fish for tuna, sardines, bonito and shark.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_mayene_3}Rakau Narrows
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_mayene_3}Rakau Narrows overlooks the hills that look towards Tear and the city of Godan. Horses graze these lush lands on the western edge of the Drowned Lands.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_mayene_1_1}Drowned Mines
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_mayene_1_1}The Drowned Mines are located in the swamps of the Drowned Lands. Villagers scrape clay from the shallow waters to sell to the region's potters.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_mayene_1_2}Aorangi Ridge
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_mayene_1_2}Aorangi Ridge sits at the foot of a range of low hills above the Drowned Lands. The villagers here sink wells to water their groves of date palms.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_mayene_2_1}Remara Bay
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_mayene_2_1}Remara Bay Village sits at the calm shoreline of Remara Bay. Here the waters are pure and the soil is fertile, and grain is grown. But a short distance to the east, the land becomes swampy and alkaline, and only the hardiest crops survive.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_mayene_2_2}Mayene Plains
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_mayene_2_2}Mayene Plains sits amid open plains and patches of dry acacia forest on the western side of the Drowned Lands. Some of the Winged Guard's most prized horses graze on the grasses of this district.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.town_tear}Tear
Settlement.text: {=4UNOQQRa}Tear is one of the oldest cities in the world, defended by the Stone of Tear which was built very shortly after the Breaking of the World. Located above the mouth of the River Erinin, its port is sheltered from the Sea of Storms.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_tear_1}The Golden Cup
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_tear_1}Named in reference to The Golden Cup inn in the city of Tear. One of many establishments Mats dice may have got him into trouble.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_tear_2}The Three Moons
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_tear_2}Named in reference to The Three Moons inn in the city of Tear. Its claim to fame is that it host the Dragon Reborn and his companions.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_tear_3}The White Crescent
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_tear_3}Named in reference to The White Crescent inn in the city of Tear. Mat and Thom stayed here on one of their exciting missions.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.town_godan}Godan
Settlement.text: {=ZAGUIKOn}Godan is a coastal town in southeast Tear that lies on the Bay of Remara across from Mayene.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_godan_1}Hogmore
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_godan_1}Hogmore lies near patchy wooded outcrops between Tear and Godan. Pigs, fed on the acorns of the southern oak trees, thrive in this climate.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_godan_2}Brenwood
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_godan_2}Brenwood sits at the base of the foothills that eventually rise to form the Spine of the World. The foresters here keep a watchful eye on their neighbors, always looking for raiding parties coming from Haddon Mirk.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_godan_3}Saltwarren
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_godan_3}Saltwarren gives its name to the narrow but deep vein of salt buried in the plains. Rumours and heresay propose the Breaking of the World is responsible for the strange find.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_tear_1_1}Swiftmeadow
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_tear_1_1}Swiftmeadow is surrounded by tall grasses and open plains, where fine horses thrive.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_tear_2_1}Heavyhills
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_tear_2_1}Heavyhills sits in the eastern hills of Tear. The land here is exceptionally rich with seams of iron in the sides of the ridges.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_tear_2_2}Potters Corner
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_tear_2_2}Potters Corner sits by the edge of the swamps by the mouth of the river Erinina. The fishing and cropping is poor in these parts but villagers collect clay from the swamps for sale to the potters of Tear and other towns.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.town_farmad}Far Madding
Settlement.text: {=YNm9vaHw}Far Madding is an independent city-state in the southeastern part of the Westlands on the Plains of Maredo. Its location makes the Far Madding an important commercial center for overland trade routes. It is the only known significant human settlement where channellers are unable to access or even sense the presence of the True Source.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_farmad_1}Woolley Knob
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_farmad_1}Woolley Knob atop a small hill on the plains of Maredo. The spring grasses sustain sizeable herds of sheep, from which the delicate wool is prized by garment makers.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_farmad_2}Yarn Creek
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_farmad_2}Yarn Creek sits in the valley of the Erinin river. The villagers, nomads who have recently settled, have learned to grow flax in this low-lying marshy land.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_farmad_3}Gnarled Rise
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_farmad_3}Gnarled Rise sits at the base of the Hills of Kintara. This low-lying country is fine land for the growing of flax.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_farmad_1_1}Kintara Ranch
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_farmad_1_1}Kintara Ranch sits west of the Far Madding City, on the plains below the Kintara Hills. Villagers here raise herds of cattle bred for cheese and milk.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_farmad_1_2}Kintara Gardens
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_farmad_1_2}Kintara Gardens lies in a sheltered area beneath the Kintara Hills. The villagers sow wheat and barley as soon as the spring arrives, taking in a good crop before the autumn.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_farmad_2_1}Trappers Run
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_farmad_2_1}Trappers Run sits between the Kintara Hills and the Chishen Mountains. The village here acts as a launching point for fur trapping runs.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_farmad_2_2}Erinin Farm
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_farmad_2_2}Erinin Farm sits in a fertile valley alongside the River Erinin.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.town_illian}Illian
Settlement.text: {=Jm2uZiwV}Marunath, the 'Horse of the Ford,' is nowhere near a river. But Battanian bards love nothing more than an incongruous place-name around which a legend can be woven. In this case the hero Ochlaigan, who was foretold to defeat the seven sons of Glanys in seven duels near seven fords, encountered the last of his enemies here. He slit his horse's throat and crossed over the stream of blood that gushed forth, defeating his last enemy and fulfiling the prophecy.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_illian_1}Millbrook
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_illian_1}Millbrook sits by the side of the Manetherendrelle, the great river that flows into the Seam of Storms. The flat, well-watered lands here are fertile and well-suited to wheat and barley.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_illian_2}Fairwater
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_illian_2}Fairwater sits on a floodplain, with the river Manetherendrelle to the west. Villagers here grow grapes to make the sweet white wine of the Illian lands.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_illian_3}Greystone
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_illian_3}Beglomuar sits in a nook of the southern escarpment of the Doirlon Hills. There is little to recommend these lands other than the ore deposits in the hills.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_illian_1_1}Goldmeadow
