Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_illian_1_1}Goldmeadow sits atop the Doirlon plateau. This is among the most fertile of the Doirlon lands, and produces a fine crops of wheat.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_illian_1_2}Whitehaven
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_illian_1_2}Whitehaven lies on the Maredo Plains. The villagers here gather stunning white clay for sale to the potters of the Illian towns.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_illian_2_1}Sterling Flats
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_illian_2_1}Sterling Flats sits upon the plains of Maredo. Villagers have sunk mines into a spur that overlooks the village, extracting silver and other precious metals.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_illian_2_2}Iron Hollows
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_illian_2_2}Iron Hollows sits in a depression on the western edge of the plains of Maredp. The villagers mine iron from shafts sunk deep into earth.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_illian_3_1}Highcrest
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_illian_3_1}Highcrest sits on a plateau region known as the Plains of Maredo. Wheat grows well on the sun-baked plains.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_illian_3_2}Brightwood
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_illian_3_2}Brightwood is a narrow ridgeline overlooking the River Erinin. Trees grow tall here in the cool shadows, and villagers harvest pines for sale as lumber.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.town_eboudar}Ebou Dar
Settlement.text: {=YERtiSUn}Ebou Dar is the capital city of Altara, located on the southern coast, straddling the River Eldar. The city is built around a large bay, with the river dividing it in two. One side contains the palaces, homes, and shops of the upper and middle classes, the other, known as the Rahad, is the home of the lower classes in Ebou Dari society.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_eboudar_1}Coramen
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_eboudar_1}Coramen sits on the edge of a plateau overlooking Ebou Dar in the distance. Villagers here tend vineyards to produce the sweet grapes required for the regions artisan wine industry.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_eboudar_2}Jurador
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_eboudar_2}Jurador is a small village of hard working men, woman and children. The villagers here are primarily foresters, supplying timber to both Salidar and the capital Ebou Dar.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_eboudar_3}Runnien Crossing
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_eboudar_3}Runnien Crossing is surrounded by farms on the hills as far as the eye can see. The region is known for its production of wheat and barley.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.town_salidar}Salidar
Settlement.text: {=X3MsaG39}Salidar sits near the River Eldar near the Amadicia border. Salidar is of some note in the history of the Aes Sedai as it was the birthplace of Deane Aryman, acknowledged as one of the greatest Amyrlins. Aryman is credited with restoring the prestige of the Aes Sedai after her predecessor Bonwhin Meraighdin severely weakened them by provoking Artur Hawkwing into besieging Tar Valon for a period of twenty years.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_salidar_1}Cormaed
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_salidar_1}Cormaed sits near the River Eldar. Villagers raise grapes on the slopes, making a strong, dark red wine highly valued across the Westlands.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_salidar_2}Ionin Spring
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_salidar_2}Ionin Spring sits in a cleft in the hills overlooking Salidar near the Damona Mountains. The villagers of these parts tend the forests, harvesting beech, oak and yew for sale to the towns.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_salidar_3}Sehar
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_salidar_3}Sehar sits on grassy downs overlooking the River Eldar and the Amadicia border. Villagers have planted flax in the boggy dales between the hills.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_altara_1_1}Moisen
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_altara_1_1}Moisen lies in heavily wooded hill country at the northern edge of Altara. Many of the villagers work as foresters, harvesting oak, beech and pine for the sale to the lake cities.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_altara_2_1}Mosra
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_altara_2_1}Mosra lies in the west of the Altaran lands near the Amadicia border. The region is relatively thinly populated, but large herds of cattle graze on the green downs.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_altara_2_2}Alkindar
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_altara_2_2}Alkindar sits at the base of an escarpment to the west of Ebou Dar. The area is rich in iron ore veins which villages mine to supply the local Altaran forges.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_altara_3_1}Soremaine
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_altara_3_1}Soremaine sits in the hills overlooking Ebou Dar in the distance to the Southeast. The people of this region have the reputation of being aloof and untrusting of outsiders, however its likely just the locals protecting their rich silver veins and mines from raiders and those how would see to exploit them.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_altara_3_2}Brytan
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_altara_3_2}Brytan is a small village with a dozen or so families. The village specializes in white grapes for the making of the sweet Altaran wines.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.town_amador}Amador
Settlement.text: {=4OAz8urz}Amador is the capital city of Amadicia. It is also where the Children of the Light have their main fortress. Even though Amador is part of Amadician Kingdom, the Amadicia king is not the true ruler, instead the Children of the Light are the ones who hold power here. Interestingly, Amador is one of the large cities in the Westlands that was neither built by Ogier nor founded on an Ogier-built city.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_amador_1}Jeramel
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_amador_1}Jeramel sits above the gorges of the upper Eldar river. Villagers scrape mud from the pools and bogs alongside the river as it levels out, carting it up the winding paths to the town of Amador perched on the cliff above them.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_amador_2}Abila
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_amador_2}Abila sits overlooking a dark wooded valley beneath the hills on the southern edge of the Mountains of Mist. Villagers farm hogs for sale to the markets of Amador.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_amador_3}Sienda
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_amador_3}Sienda sits on an outcrop overlooking a waygate near the Shadow Coast Hills. The villages carve out an existance mining the iron-rich ores of the nearby hills.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_amadicia_1_1}Bellon
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_amadicia_1_1}Bellon sits a plateau above the upper Amador river that eventually joins the Andahar River near Tanchico. The villagers of this region grow grapes on the hillsides, used to make a white wine popular with the local aristocracies.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_amadicia_1_2}Nassad
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_amadicia_1_2}Nassad sits nestled into a steep ridge above the Amador River. The surrounding hillsides are rich with silver deposits.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_amadicia_2_1}Sharia
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_amadicia_2_1}Sharia sits in the foothills of the Shadow Coast. The villagers, known as skilled trappers, run their trap lines deep into the ranges.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_amadicia_2_2}Gaean
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_amadicia_2_2}Gaean sits on the plateau south of Amador. The lands is not especially fertile here, but pigs thrive in the bush and shrubland of the heights.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.town_tanchico}Tanchico
Settlement.text: {=79R6gi4l}Tanchico, one of the oldest cities in the World and capital of Tarabon, sits near the mouth of the River Andahar overlooking Tanchico Bay. Tanchico is the home of both the Tarabon King and Panarch, and also the home to the Society known as the Guild of Illuminators.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_tanchico_1}Raysina
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_tanchico_1}Raysina sits on a shelf overlooking the River Andahar. The climate is warm most of the year, and grapes do well on the slopes of the nearby hills.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_tanchico_2}Janul
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_tanchico_2}Janul is a fishing village overlooking a cove in the Tarabon Coast. The villagers sail out into the western ocean in search of mullet and bass near the shore, and tuna and sardines amid the islands.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_tanchico_3}Mahina
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_tanchico_3}Mahina sits on the Tarabon coastline northwest of Tanchico. Villagers here raise sheep on the pastures between the coastal woods and the high crags of the hills.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.town_elmora}Elmora
Settlement.text: {=Eb7Lgq10}Elmora is located between Tanchico and Amador and benefits from this location as a stop over for caravans and traders.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_elmora_1}Surar
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_elmora_1}Surar known for its horses, who graze in rocky slopes flecked with primrose and daffodils.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_elmora_2}Bahico
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_elmora_2}Bahico lies in the wet wooded hills of the Mountains of Mist, where cedars grow tall in the valley-mists. The village is known for producing fine pigs, who are allowed to range through the thick woods rooting for acorns, chestnuts and grubs.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_elmora_3}Mahis
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_elmora_3}Mahis sits between the River Andahar and River Amador. Villagers can then range the Mountians of Mist on both sides of the Andahar gorge to trap fur-bearing animals like marten, rabbit and fox. This remote region has recently attracted many settlers drawn to the relative freedom of the woods, where the rule of the nobles is much lighter than in the east.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_tarabon_1_1}Almara
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_tarabon_1_1}Almara sits in the shelter of the Tanchico Bay, a calm bay just south of the mouth of the River Andahar. The villagers venture out far into the seas to cod-rich banks washed by a cold current from the north.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_tarabon_1_2}Haralus
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_tarabon_1_2}Haralus sits high on a plateau near the Shadow Coast. The local landowners raise horses in paddocks of lush plain grasses.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_tarabon_2_1}Alcruna
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_tarabon_2_1}Alcruna sits to the south of the Almoth plains in Tarabon. The hills above the town are lined with vineyards which thrive in the well-drained, sandy soil.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.town_bandar}Bandar Eban
Settlement.text: {=0cZmKO76}Bandar Eban is the capital of Arad Doman and the prime port city of the northwest. It is located on the coast of the Aryth Ocean. Arandi Square, the main city square, contains copper fountains in the shape of horses leaping from a frothy wave, while the Terhana Library is considered to be among the best in the world.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_bandar_1}Kashan
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_bandar_1}Kashan sits on warm, grassy plains north of Bandar Eban. The land is fine pasture for the renowned horses of the Arad Domani.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_bandar_2}Maymand
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_bandar_2}The village of Maymand lies on the coast of the Aryth Ocean. This warm land is well-suited to the growing of olive trees.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_bandar_3}Darab
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_bandar_3}Darab sits on the green hills north of Bandar Eban. The surrounding farmlands are used by local farmers to use hogs for market.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.town_katar}Katar
Settlement.text: {=QD3oaaNK}Katar lies just west of the Mountains of Mist and northwest of Lake Somal. It is claimed by by Arad Doman, although its remoteness from Bandar Eban ensures a level of autonomy one could think of it as a city-state in its own right that exists outside of Domani territory. With the Seanchan threat to the south, rumours of the Dragon Reborn to the east and Trollocs to the North, it makes strategic sense to allow Domani alliegences to thrive.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_katar_1}Gorgan
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_katar_1}Gorgan sits in an isolated valley to the west of Katar. Villagers raise hogs as the marshy windswept location suits little else.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_katar_2}Semnan
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_katar_2}Semnan is a fur-trapping village near the site of a duel famous in Domani legend. The villagers are known for their skill as foresters, helping the nearby settlement of Katar to guard the eastern approaches to the Almoth Plain heartland from raiders.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_katar_3}Kerman
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_katar_3}Kerman lies beneath a wooded knoll near the southern Saldaean lands. Villagers harvest the thick-growing forests of oak, yew and beech. The woods here have been tended carefully by the Domani for centuries, ensuring that there are always enough trees to harvest and sell for timber.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_doman_1_1}Maseen
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_doman_1_1}Maseen sits on a plateau, facing the Mountains of Mist. It is a land of cold winds and constant low-level warfare, cris-crossed by Arad Domani and Tarabon raiding parties. The villagers raise hogs here, always listening to the warning horns telling them to drive their herds to hiding spots in the hills.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_doman_2_1}Urmia
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_doman_2_1}Urmia sits on a flat shelf of land on the Almoth Plains. The land is cold, but the runoff from the hills brings rich soil, and villagers grow hardy crops like rye and barley.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_doman_2_2}Coron Ford
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_doman_2_2}Coron Ford sits on pastures overlooking the Aryth Ocean. Horses graze on the heights, glancing down occasionally at the trading longships and fishing boats passing beneath them.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.town_falme}Falme
Settlement.text: {=Nx0qK2g6}Falme is the largest town on Toman Head and Almoth Plain, with a harbour on the Aryth Ocean. The town is founded on the site of the ancient city of Miereallen and was the original landing point for the Seanchan's return.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_falme_1}Atueh's Orchard
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_falme_1}Atueh's Orchard sits on the Ayrth Ocean coast. The soil is thin in these parts, but the cool sea air that flows inland and the warm afternoons allow the growing of wine grapes.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_falme_2}Tobin's Hollow
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_falme_2}Tobin's Hollow sits at the top of Tomans Head. The villagers specialize in the raising of hogs, who feast on acorns from the oak woodlands.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_falme_3}Atuans Mill
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_falme_3}Atuans Mill sits on plains just outside of Falme. Villagers plant rye, barley and wheat for sale to the hungry urban population of nearby Falme.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.town_darluna}Darluna
Settlement.text: {=aOn6pa0D}Darluna is a large town in southern Arad Doman that quickly fell to the advancing Seanchan forces upon their arrival. The citizens have embraced their new rulers traditions and customs, and the wealth that comes with them.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_darluna_1}Fern Hollow
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_darluna_1}Fern Hollow on a flat plain between the Ayrth Ocean and Mountains of Mist. The people of this district grow flax on the wet lands.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_darluna_2}Darluna Mill
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_darluna_2}Darluna Mill sits on the Almoth Plains. The villagers here grow wheat, finding a ready market in the garrisons stationed nearby.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.village_darluna_3}Moss Haven
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.village_darluna_3}Moss Haven sits in the foothills beneath the Mountains of Mist. Villagers take clay from the damp soil for sale to the potters of nearby towns.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_seachan_1_1}Ochre Mines
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_seachan_1_1}Ochre Mines sits in a rift valley on the Almoth Plains. Miners here orignally speculated on iron deposits - which gives origin to the Ochre Mines name, but have found silver in clefts in the hillsides instead.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_seachan_2_1}Twig Mill
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_seachan_2_1}Twig Mill sits in the lush lands of the Almoth Plains. This land, rich in wheat is the bread basket of the Seanchan kingdom.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_seachan_2_2}Olive Fall
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_seachan_2_2}Olive Fall sits on the warm Almoth Plains far enough from the Mountains of Mist to provide tree cropping. The villagers here grow olives on the warm, dry landward side of the hills.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_seachan_3_1}Green Harvest
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_seachan_3_1}Green Harvest lies on the misty coastal plain near the Ayrth Ocean. Villagers grow flax in the lowlands here.

Settlement.name: {=Settlements.Settlement.name.castle_village_seachan_3_2}Stybend Farm
Settlement.text: {=Settlements.Settlement.text.castle_village_seachan_3_2}Stybend Farm sits on the Almoth Plain. Villagers here raise hogs to support the local garrisons.

Settlement.name: {=8dDNWHR4}The Retreat
Settlement.text: {=E3rYg96K}This is the retirement retreat location.
